news.headline: Beirut airport remains operational despite Israeli bombings
news.summary: Despite Israeli airstrikes in the area, Beirut airport remains operational. Kept open through close coordination and international guarantees, it is operating at reduced capacity.

news.headline: Russia and Ukraine trade strikes before temporary ceasefire
news.summary: An Orthodox Easter ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine began on Saturday. The Kremlin said it had ordered a 32-hour truce from 4pm (13:00 GMT) to midnight on Sunday, a week after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky proposed a halt to hostilities for the holiday. news.headline: U.S.-Iran Talks Timeline: Key Moments Between Two Adversaries
news.summary: From sanctions relief to nuclear talks, here’s a look at how negotiations between Washington and Tehran have gone over the decades.

news.headline: Israel army says hit over 200 Hezbollah targets in last 24 hours
news.summary: Clouds of smoke were once again seen over villages in southern Lebanon after Israeli strikes targeted areas near the border. news.headline: Iran, US top officials meet face-to-face
news.summary: The United States and Iran began negotiations Saturday in Pakistan, days after a fragile two-week ceasefire was announced, as the war that has killed thousands of people and shaken global markets entered its seventh week. FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar tells us more.

news.headline: Overlooked No More: Margaret Gipsy Moth, Fearless CNN Camera Operator
news.summary: She covered conflicts across the globe, joking after she was injured in Sarajevo that she would be returning to find her missing teeth.

news.headline: Iran Has Been Consistent in War. Will It Be Consistent in Peace Talks?
news.summary: While President Trump’s war aims have changed by the moment, Iran has stuck to firm demands. The question is whether it will compromise in peace negotiations.

news.headline: Paris marathon marks its 50th year
news.summary: Celebrating its 50th anniversary this year, the Paris Marathon sees participants staying connected throughout the race, with technology playing a key role in managing exertion and optimizing performance. Caitlin Brown reports.

news.headline: Mexico seeks to curb reliance on US gas
news.summary: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has announced plans to use new hydraulic fracturing technologies, or fracking, to reduce Mexico’s reliance on foreign energy amid the Iran war.
